title: 2 Key Market Events That Could Move Stocks This Month
author: Jennifer Saibil
description: Two major market events could drive significant stock movements this week: the Federal Reserve's interest rate decision on Wednesday, where no rate changes are expected but guidance could surprise markets, and SpaceX's potential inclusion in major indexes like the Russell 1000 and Nasdaq-100, which could trigger substantial trading volume in related ETFs.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: SpaceX is positioned for potential inclusion in major indexes (Russell 1000, Nasdaq-100) which could drive significant trading volume and market attention. The IPO was described as the largest ever, and index inclusion could result in wide market moves as ETFs rebalance.
Mentioned as a tracker ETF that would be affected by SpaceX inclusion in the Russell 1000. The impact is mechanical and dependent on index changes rather than fundamental performance.
Similar to other Russell 1000 tracking ETFs, would be affected by SpaceX inclusion but the impact is procedural rather than based on underlying fundamentals.
Another Russell 1000 tracker ETF that would mechanically adjust holdings if SpaceX is included, with neutral sentiment based on index rebalancing.
Could potentially include SpaceX within 15 days if it qualifies for Nasdaq-100. The impact would be from index inclusion mechanics rather than fundamental factors.

title: Large-Cap ETF (IWB) Hits New 52-Week High
author: Sweta Killa
description: This large-cap ETF hits a new 52-week high. Are more gains in store for this ETF?

title: 5 Charts Expose Troubling Weaknesses In Record-High Stock Market: 'This Is Not Normal'
author: Piero Cingari
description:
As the S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 indices continue to notch new all-time highs, it’s clear that the rising tide has not lifted all boats.
Sharp-eyed analysts and investors have noted some discordant features in a stock market at its peak, raising questions about the euphoria that currently reigns supreme on Wall Street.
Mega-Cap Dominance Unprecedented Since Late 2008
The dominance of mega-cap stocks hasn’t been this pronounced in nearly sixteen years. 
The top 100 U.S. stocks by market capitalization, monitored through the iShares S&P 100 ETF (NYSE:OEF), have never been stronger against the Russell 1000 Index, as tracked by the iShares Russell 1000 ETF (NYSE:IWB), since December 2008-.

David Morrison, senior market Analyst at Trade Nation, commented, “There are concerns about the US market's lack of breadth with so much of the overall performance concentrated in the stock price of a few corporations. In fact, the five biggest companies by market capitalization in the S&P 500 account for nearly 30% of its value. The size and ...Full story available on Benzinga.com

title: ETFs Worth a Look on Russell Indexes' Shake-Up
author: Sweta Killa
description: The popular Russell indexes are set to get the customary new look this month. ETFs targeting the Russell Indices are currently in focus.

title: Airbnb, Norwegian Cruise See Analyst Upgrades: Are Travel And Leisure Stocks The New Value Heaven?
author: Piero Cingari
description:
Shares in the travel and leisure industry are increasingly catching the eye of value investors, presenting discounted valuations alongside promising growth prospects for the upcoming tourist season.
Since the start of the current bull-market rally, major industry exchange-traded funds (ETFs) tracking travel and leisure companies have underperformed relative to the broader stock market.
The Defiance Hotel, Airline, and Cruise ETF (NYSE:CRUZ), the Alps Global Travel Beneficiaries ETF (NYSE:JRNY), and the AdvisorShares Hotel ETF (NYSE:BEDZ) have gained 28%, 24.5%, and 22.8%, respectively, compared to a 29% surge in the SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (NYSE:SPY) since late October 2023.
Yet, the outlook for travel-related stocks is improving, drawing interest from traders looking to invest in underperforming sectors amid the broader bull market.
Chart: Travel Stocks Lagged Behind The Broader S&P 500 In The Current Bull-Market Rally
